_readme: Verified official broadcast-channel registry (Roadmap Phase C1).
Discovery prefers KNOWN official channels and their upload playlists
over broad YouTube search (C2), because videos.list costs 1 quota unit
while search.list is ~100x more expensive (C4). Broad search is a
fallback only.
Each channel scores candidates during broadcast matching (C3): an
official channel adds authority, an unofficial one is penalised. Only
high-confidence candidates auto-link; medium-confidence ones enter
review. `channelId` is null until the real YouTube channel id is
confirmed; a null-id channel is kept disabled so it never drives
discovery on a guess.
`priority` breaks ties when multiple official feeds exist for one match
(higher wins). `allowedEventTypes` restricts a channel to the event
families it actually broadcasts. `language`/`region` feed the preferred
feed selection (Phase E4): one calibrated-layout feed is captured, not
every language.
New Phase C1 fields on every entry:
sourceUrl          candidate/confirmed official channel URL (evidence,
NOT a channel id — ids only come from the live API)
ownershipEvidence   why this URL is believed to be the official channel
verifiedDate        last UTC date channelId was confirmed via the
YouTube Data API channels.list (null = never verified)
verifiedStatus      unverified | verified | stale | failed
disabledReason      explicit reason an entry stays disabled (required
whenever enabled=false so a gap is never silent)
preferredLayout     optional calibrated broadcast-layout id (Phase E4);
null until a layout exists for this feed
VERIFICATION STATUS (2026-07-24): no channelId has been confirmed via a
live YouTube Data API call yet — this registry pass ran without network
access to the YouTube API (no key in this environment by design; the key
only exists as a GitHub Actions secret). `ow_esports_global` carries a
sourceUrl evidenced from Liquipedia's OWCS page (which lists
https://www.youtube.com/OW_Esports alongside the official Twitch/
Discord/social accounts, credited to Blizzard Entertainment). Every
other regional entry has NO evidenced channel URL from sources reachable
this pass, so it stays disabled with an explicit reason rather than a
guess. Run `cli.py verify-channels` (or the discovery workflow's
`mode=verify-channels` dispatch, where YOUTUBE_API_KEY lives) to resolve
real channel ids from these sourceUrls and flip verifiedStatus.

channels.name: Overwatch Esports (Global English)
channels.ownershipEvidence: Listed as the official YouTube channel on Liquipedia's Overwatch Champions Series page, alongside the official Twitch (twitch.tv/OW_Esports), Discord, Instagram and X/Twitter accounts, credited to Blizzard Entertainment in partnership with FACEIT/WDG/NetEase.
channels.disabledReason: channelId not yet confirmed via a live YouTube Data API channels.list call (requires YOUTUBE_API_KEY, only available as a GitHub Actions secret). Run verify-channels in Actions to resolve.

channels.name: Overwatch Esports (Korea)
channels.disabledReason: no regional-specific official YouTube channel could be evidenced from sources reachable this pass. Do not guess a separate channel id; the global channel (ow_esports_global) may itself carry Korea broadcasts once verified. Needs a human-sourced official URL before verify-channels can resolve an id.

channels.name: Overwatch Esports (Japan)
channels.disabledReason: no regional-specific official YouTube channel could be evidenced from sources reachable this pass. Do not guess a separate channel id. Needs a human-sourced official URL before verify-channels can resolve an id.

channels.name: OWCS Pacific
channels.disabledReason: no regional-specific official YouTube channel could be evidenced from sources reachable this pass. Do not guess a separate channel id. Needs a human-sourced official URL before verify-channels can resolve an id.

channels.name: OWCS China
channels.disabledReason: platform is bilibili, not YouTube — out of scope for the Phase C2 YouTube Data API client. This entry is kept in the registry so coverage reporting can show China as an explicit 'unsupported-source' region rather than a silent gap; it will never be driven by the YouTube discovery layer.
